_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 10-15 SPRN Ambulance Cleaning and Disinfection (MDHHS Reviewed 2022-12-27). MCA Optional — SPRN program participation required.
protocol_reference: Michigan Special Operations Protocol Section 10-15: SPRN Ambulance Cleaning and Disinfection — MDHHS Reviewed 2022
purpose: Define procedures for decontamination and disinfection of SPRN transport vehicles after transport of patients with High Consequence Infectious Diseases (HCIDs).

sections.title: General Principles
content: SPRN ambulance decontamination requires EPA-registered hospital disinfectant with DEMONSTRATED EFFICACY against non-enveloped viruses
Non-enveloped virus claim = highest tier of EPA disinfectant efficacy — suitable for HCID pathogens including filoviruses (Ebola/Marburg)
Standard ambulance disinfection protocols are INSUFFICIENT for SPRN — must use appropriate EPA-registered product
Vehicle is OUT OF SERVICE from time of patient contact until decontamination is certified complete
Decontamination team must wear full SPRN PPE (Protocol 10-12) during cleaning

sections.title: Decontamination Zone Setup
content: Establish THREE ZONES for decontamination operations:
— HOT ZONE: inside the ambulance patient compartment — most contaminated; requires full SPRN PPE
— WARM ZONE: immediately outside ambulance doors — transition area; requires full SPRN PPE
— COLD ZONE: staging and clean equipment area; minimal PPE required
Clearly delineate zones with physical markers or tape
All waste (Biocell/Visquine covers, MITD components, PPE, supplies) removed from hot zone before cleaning begins
Remove and dispose of Biocell/Visquine patient containment covers as Category A biological waste before surface cleaning

sections.title: Cleaning and Disinfection — Sequential Steps
content: STEP 1 — REMOVE VISIBLE CONTAMINATION:
— Remove all patient care supplies, equipment, used PPE, biohazard waste from patient compartment
— Place in biohazard bags; seal and label as Category A biological waste
— Remove Biocell or Visquine covers from MITD if used
STEP 2 — CLEAN (remove organic material before disinfecting):
— Wipe all surfaces with detergent solution to remove organic material (blood, secretions, body fluids)
— Work from ENTRANCE to DEEPEST/DIRTIEST area (entrance to rear/patient area = dirty direction)
— Use disposable wipes or cloths — dispose as biohazard waste after use
STEP 3 — DISINFECT (apply EPA-registered non-enveloped virus disinfectant):
— Apply to ALL surfaces: walls, ceiling, floor, handles, equipment mounts, oxygen fittings, seat cushions
— Follow manufacturer DWELL TIME (contact time) — typically 5-10 minutes; do NOT wipe off early
— Re-apply to any surface that dries before dwell time is met
STEP 4 — FINAL WIPE AND DRY:
— Wipe surfaces after dwell time with clean disposable cloths
— Allow vehicle to air dry with doors open
STEP 5 — OPTIONAL TERMINAL DISINFECTION METHODS (as available/authorized):
— UV-C germicidal irradiation: whole-ambulance UV cycle per device protocol
— Chlorine dioxide gas: facility-level treatment; specialized team required
— Hydrogen peroxide vapor: vaporized H2O2 system; specialized team required
— These methods supplement but do NOT replace Steps 1-4

sections.title: Category A Biological Waste
content: All waste from SPRN transport and decontamination is CATEGORY A BIOLOGICAL WASTE
Category A: UN 2814 — infectious substance affecting humans; regulated by DOT, IATA, and Michigan DEQ
Handling requirements:
— Double-bag in red biohazard bags; place in rigid outer container; label UN 2814
— Do NOT dispose of in standard medical waste containers or municipal waste
— Coordinate with hospital, state, or contracted medical waste vendor for Category A disposal
— MDHHS or BEPESOC can provide guidance on Category A waste disposal for unusual situations
Sharps from SPRN transport: sharps container (labeled HCID/Category A), then into outer biohazard bag

sections.title: Return to Service
content: Vehicle may NOT return to service until decontamination is certified complete
Certification: documented by EMS supervisor or safety officer reviewing completed decon checklist
Restock with clean supplies per Protocol 10-12 supply list before next deployment
Notify MCA medical director and agency supervisor of decontamination completion
Document decontamination: date, time, disinfectant product name/lot number, crew performing decon, dwell time achieved

mca_selections_required.label: SPRN Program Participation — MCA authorization for SPRN protocols 10-10 through 10-18
mca_selections_required.description: MCA Optional: All SPRN protocols apply only to SPRN-designated agencies.

deterioration_flags: Disinfectant product does not have non-enveloped virus EPA claim — do NOT use for SPRN decontamination; contact agency for appropriate product
Visible contamination (body fluids) found after initial cleaning pass — repeat cleaning step before re-disinfecting
scope_notes: MCA Optional — SPRN only. Must use EPA-registered disinfectant with non-enveloped virus claim — standard ambulance disinfectants are insufficient. Dwell time must be achieved before wiping. All SPRN waste is Category A (UN 2814) biological waste — not standard medical waste. Optional terminal disinfection methods (UV, chlorine dioxide, H2O2 vapor) supplement but do not replace manual cleaning.
